description: v2 positive (model-generation) tasks; the 2 should_fail negatives are scored by tools/aether_negative_tier.py

tasks.title: Minimal fx hello world
tasks.prompt: Write the smallest valid Aether program that prints exactly `hello from benchmark` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Constants plus fx output
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that defines `Greeting` as `hello` and `Target` as `Aether`, then prints exactly `hello, Aether` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Pure helper plus effectful output
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a pure helper `classify(score: Int) -> Text` that returns `ready` for scores >= 90, `review` for scores >= 70, and `blocked` otherwise. In `main`, print three lines for scores 95, 72, and 10 in the format `95 => ready`.

tasks.title: Half-open range loop
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that uses a `loop i in 0..5` style range loop and prints one line per iteration in the form `0 => 0`, `1 => 1`, ..., `4 => 16`.

tasks.title: Conditional loop with break
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that starts `level` at 0 and `steps` at 0. Using a loop, repeatedly add 7 to `level` and add 1 to `steps`, printing `step N: LEVEL` each iteration (e.g. `step 1: 7`). Stop the loop with `break` the first time `level` is at least 30, then print `steps = S`.
tasks.expected_stdout:
step 1: 7
step 2: 14
step 3: 21
step 4: 28
step 5: 35
steps = 5

tasks.title: Nested loops over a grid
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with nested range loops over `i` and `j`, each from 1 to 3 inclusive, printing one line per pair in the form `i x j = product` (e.g. `2 x 3 = 6`).

tasks.title: Inline conditional expression
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that computes `avg0` from `total0 = 9` and `count0 = 0`, and `avg2` from `total2 = 9` and `count2 = 2`, using inline `if ... else ...` expressions on the right-hand side. Print `avg0=0` and `avg2=4` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Real arithmetic with formatted output
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with `const PI: Real = 3.14159265;` and a pure helper `area(radius: Real) -> Real`. Use radius `2.5` and print exactly `area=19.63` followed by a newline. Use Aether's real formatting syntax instead of string post-processing.

tasks.title: Real percentage formatting
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that computes a success percentage from `ok = 3` and `total = 4`, forces real arithmetic correctly, and prints exactly `pct=75.00` followed by a newline using Aether's real-formatting syntax.

tasks.title: Boolean-operator predicate
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a pure helper `admit(age: Int, hasTicket: Bool, isVip: Bool, flagged: Bool) -> Bool` that returns true when age is at least 18 AND (the guest has a ticket OR is a VIP) AND is NOT flagged, using the boolean operators `&&`, `||`, and `!`. In `main`, print `guest N: RESULT` for guests (20,true,false,false), (16,true,false,false), (25,false,false,false), (30,false,true,true), (40,true,false,false).
tasks.expected_stdout:
guest 0: true
guest 1: false
guest 2: false
guest 3: false
guest 4: true

tasks.title: String length and equality checks
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a pure helper `validName(name: Text) -> Bool` that returns true only when the name length is between 1 and 6 inclusive and the name is not equal to `bad`. In `main`, print `alpha=true`, `empty=false`, and `bad=false` on separate lines for the sample inputs `alpha`, ``, and `bad`.

tasks.title: Record-style type initialization
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that defines a `Point` type with `x` and `y` integer fields and a `sum() -> Int` method. Initialize a point with values 3 and 4 using current Aether type-initialization syntax, and print exactly `sum=7` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Stateful type with status method
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that defines an `Inventory` type with `currentStock` and `maxCapacity` fields, an `addItem(amount: Int) -> Int` method that updates the stock without exceeding capacity, and a `getStatus() -> Text` method that returns `FULL`, `IN_STOCK`, or `EMPTY`. Initialize the inventory to 10 of 50, add 25, and print `Final Stock: 35` and `Current Status: IN_STOCK` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Two custom types with extension methods
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with two custom types: a `Point` with integer `x` and `y`, and a `Box` with integer `width` and `height`. Give `Point` an extension method `shiftX(dx: Int) -> Int` returning `x + dx`, and `Box` an extension method `area() -> Int` returning `width * height`. In `main`, create `Point` (3,4) and `Box` (5,6), then print `shiftedX=` (shiftX 10), `area=`, and `combined=` (shiftX 10 + area) on three lines.

tasks.title: Tuple return plus destructuring
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a helper `pair() -> (Text, Int)` that returns `("answer", 42)`. Destructure the direct call with `let (label, value) = pair();` and print `answer 42` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Tuple return with @post on a slot
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a helper `ordered(a: Int, b: Int) -> (Int, Int)` that returns the two inputs as a tuple with the larger value first, and an `@post` contract asserting the first slot is >= the second (use positional slot syntax). In `main`, destructure `ordered(3, 8)` into `hi` and `lo` and print `hi=8` and `lo=3` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Dynamic array length and indexing
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that creates an `Int[]` dynamic array containing the values 3, 1, 4, 1, and 5 using supported array syntax. Iterate over it with `length(...)` and indexing, compute the sum and maximum value, and print `sum=14`, `max=5`, and `count=5` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Array min/max/range scan
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that declares an `Int[]` array containing `12, 5, 23, 8, 19`, then scans it with `length(...)` and indexing to find the minimum, maximum, and range (max - min). Print `min = `, `max = `, and `range = ` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Preconditions and postconditions
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with `@pre` and `@post` contracts on a helper `normalize(score: Int) -> Int` that clamps a non-negative score into the range 0..100. Call it for 150 and 42, and print `150 => 100` and `42 => 42` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Import and call a module function
tasks.prompt: A module file named `bench_math` is provided in the working directory. It exports a function `cube(n: Int) -> Int`. Write an Aether program that imports the module using current Aether import syntax and, by calling the exported function, prints `cube(2)=8`, `cube(3)=27`, and `cube(4)=64` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Import and use module constants
tasks.prompt: A module file named `bench_consts` is provided in the working directory. It exports constants `Base` (100) and `Step` (7). Write an Aether program that imports the module using current Aether import syntax and, using the exported constants, prints `Base`, `Base + Step`, and `Base + Step + Step` on three lines (`100`, `107`, `114`).

tasks.title: TOON parsing with inline node lookup
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`. If TOON support is missing, print `toon unavailable` and exit. Otherwise parse the JSON text `{\"name\":\"Aether\",\"score\":42}`, extract the `name` and `score`, print them as `Aether 42`, close the document, and return.

tasks.title: TOON parse_file with typed extraction
tasks.prompt: A file named `payload.json` is provided in the working directory. Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file with `toon_parse_file`, extracts `name`, `enabled`, and `count`, prints those three values on separate lines, closes the document, and returns.

tasks.title: TOON value type introspection
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the inline TOON text `{"values":[42, "hi", true, 3.5]}`, iterates the `values` array, and for each element prints `item N: KIND` where KIND is `int`, `text`, `bool`, or `real`, determined using Aether's TOON type-probing helpers rather than guessing. Close the document.
tasks.expected_stdout:
item 0: int
item 1: text
item 2: bool
item 3: real

tasks.title: Safe nested TOON lookups with missing data
tasks.prompt: A file named `rows.json` is provided in the working directory. Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, iterates through the `rows` array, safely reads `meta.code` when present, falls back to `EMPTY` when it is absent, prints `row 0 = A1` style lines, counts how many rows resolved to `EMPTY`, and prints `missing = N` at the end. Use the safer staged TOON access pattern rather than assuming nested fallbacks protect the whole chain.
tasks.expected_stdout:
row 0 = A1
row 1 = EMPTY
row 2 = EMPTY
missing = 2

tasks.title: Deeply nested TOON (array of objects of arrays)
tasks.prompt: A file named `rosters.json` is provided with shape `{"teams":[{"name":Text,"members":[Text,...]}]}`. Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, and for each team prints `team N: NAME / COUNT` where COUNT is the number of entries in that team's nested `members` array, then prints `total members = T`. Close the document.
tasks.expected_stdout:
team 0: Reds / 3
team 1: Blues / 2
total members = 5

tasks.title: Real-valued mean with fixed precision
tasks.prompt: Given three integer scores 70, 80, and 95, print exactly two lines. First line: `total = <sum>` where <sum> is their sum. Second line: `average = <mean>` where <mean> is their arithmetic mean rounded to exactly two decimal places.

tasks.title: Clamp values into a range
tasks.prompt: Print, one per line and in this order, each of these values clamped to the inclusive range 0 to 100: first 120, then -5, then 50.

tasks.title: Count pass/fail by threshold
tasks.prompt: Four students scored 75, 50, 90, and 40. A score of 60 or above passes; below 60 fails. Print `passed = <count>` on the first line and `failed = <count>` on the second.

tasks.title: Recursive factorial
tasks.prompt: Using a recursive function, compute the factorial of 5 and print it exactly in the form `5! = <value>`.
